title: Current account of the balance of payments
paperName: Cambridge International AS Level Economics (P1 & P2)
summary: 9708 AS balance of payments — trade balance, income flows, and links to AD with GeoGebra macro diagram.
learningObjectives: Define components of the current account.
Calculate balance of trade in goods and services.
Explain causes of current account deficits and surpluses.
Link net exports to aggregate demand.

simpleExplanation.title: Recording international flows
simpleExplanation.summary: The current account records trade in goods and services plus primary and secondary income. (X − M) is a component of AD.
steps: Balance of trade = exports of goods − imports of goods.
Deficit: more spending abroad than income from abroad.
Financed by capital/financial account inflows (or reserves).

sections.content: **Current account** (6.3) connects to **exchange rates** (6.4) and **corrective policies** (6.5). Memorise the **four components** and **sign conventions**.

sections.label: Mark a balance of payments question

steps.caption: Current account = trade in goods + services + primary + secondary income.
steps.embedHint: X − M on goods is balance of trade.

steps.caption: Deficit: spending abroad > earnings; surplus opposite.
steps.embedHint: Link net exports to AD component (X−M).

steps.caption: Causes: exchange rate, competitiveness, growth differentials.
Sustainability: financing via capital/financial account flows.
steps.embedHint: CA + KA + FA balances with errors/omissions.
